questions.to_find: Array contents of the max-heap after two delete-max operations

understand.plain: We start with a max-heap stored as an array: [25, 14, 16, 13, 10, 8, 12]. A delete operation removes the maximum element (root), replaces it with the last element, then restores the heap property by sifting down. We perform this twice and record the resulting array.

keywords.explain: Remove the root (largest element), place the last array element at the root, reduce size by 1, then sift the new root down by swapping with the larger child until heap property is restored.
keywords.example: Deleting from [10, 7, 9]: move 9 to root → [9, 7], done.

keywords.explain: Starting at a node, compare it with its children; if a child is larger, swap and continue downward until no violation exists.
For a 1-indexed array, the children of node i are at 2i and 2i+1; the parent of i is at floor(i/2).
keywords.example: Array [25, 14, 16, ...]: root=25, left child of root=14, right child=16.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='520' height='280' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe x2='340' y2='95' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='180' y1='95' x2='140' y2='145' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='180' y1='95' x2='220' y2='145' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='340' y1='95' x2='300' y2='145' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='340' y1='95' x2='380' y2='145' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='260' cy='50' r='22' class='no'/><text x='260' y='56' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl' style='fill:#fff'>25</text><circle cx='180' cy='95' r='22' class='bg'/><text x='180' y='101' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>14</text><circle cx='340' cy='95' r='22' class='bg'/><text x='340' y='101' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>16</text><circle cx='140' cy='145' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='140' y='151' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>13</text><circle cx='220' cy='145' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='220' y='151' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>10</text><circle cx='300' cy='145' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='300' y='151' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>8</text><circle cx='380' cy='145' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='380' y='151' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>12</text><text x='10' y='210' class='tag ink'>After 1st delete (25): [16,14,12,13,10,8]</text><text x='10' y='235' class='tag ink'>After 2nd delete (16): [14,13,12,8,10]</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Binary max-heap tree with root 25, children 14 and 16. After two delete-max operations, the resulting heap array is [14, 13, 12, 8, 10].

given.aim: Find the array contents of the max-heap after performing two delete-max operations

terms.example: 25 is the root (maximum), its children are 14 and 16

terms.meaning: Removes the maximum (root), replaces it with the last element, then sifts down to restore heap property
terms.example: Deleting from [5, 3, 4]: move 4 to root → [4, 3], done
terms.connects: Applied twice to get the final array

terms.meaning: Swap the current node with its largest child if that child is greater, repeat until heap property holds
terms.example: Node 2 with children 7 and 5: swap with 7 → node 7 with child 2
terms.connects: Used after each deletion to restore the max-heap invariant

given.plan: Perform the first delete-max: remove 25, place 12 at root, sift down. Then perform the second delete-max: remove the new root, place the new last element at root, sift down again. Record the resulting array.

steps.title: First delete-max: remove 25
steps.formula_raw: Replace root with last element, sift down
steps.apply: Initial: [25, 14, 16, 13, 10, 8, 12]. Remove 25, move last element 12 to root → [12, 14, 16, 13, 10, 8]. Sift down 12: children are 14 (index 2) and 16 (index 3); largest child = 16. Swap 12 and 16 → [16, 14, 12, 13, 10, 8]. Now 12 is at index 3 (0-indexed: index 2), its children in 1-indexed array are at positions 5 and 6 which are 8 and nothing relevant — actually node at position 3 (1-indexed) has children at 6 and 7: values 8 and (out of bounds after removal). 8 < 12, no swap needed. Result: [16, 14, 12, 13, 10, 8].
steps.note: After first delete, heap has 6 elements: [16, 14, 12, 13, 10, 8].

steps.title: Second delete-max: remove 16
steps.formula_raw: Replace root with last element, sift down
steps.apply: Current heap: [16, 14, 12, 13, 10, 8]. Remove 16, move last element 8 to root → [8, 14, 12, 13, 10]. Sift down 8: children are 14 (pos 2) and 12 (pos 3); largest = 14. Swap 8 and 14 → [14, 8, 12, 13, 10]. Now 8 is at position 2; its children are 13 (pos 4) and 10 (pos 5). Largest child = 13 > 8, swap → [14, 13, 12, 8, 10]. Node 8 is now at position 4; its children would be at positions 8 and 9, which are out of bounds. Sift-down complete. Result: [14, 13, 12, 8, 10].
steps.note: Final heap has 5 elements. Option D matches [14, 13, 12, 8, 10].

solution.result: The array after two delete operations is {14, 13, 12, 8, 10} — option D.

questions.formula_note: Max-heap delete-max: replace root with last element, reduce size, apply sift-down (heapify-down) to restore heap property.

questions.question:
A max-heap is a heap where the value of each parent is greater than or equal to the values of its children. Which of the following is a max-heap?

A. A tree with root 97, left child 17 (with left child 6 and right child 3), right child 46 (with left child 12 and right child 10)
B. A tree with root 97, left child 17 (with left child 6), right child 46 (with left child 12 and right child 10), and 6 has a right child 7
C. A tree with root 97, left child 46 (with left child 6 and right child 10), right child 17 (with left child 12 and right child 5)
D. A tree with root 97, left child 46, right child 17 (with left child 5), and 46 has left child 12 and right child 10 and 17 has right child 12
questions.answer: A. A tree with root 97, left child 17 (with left child 6 and right child 3), right child 46 (with left child 12 and right child 10)
questions.to_find: Which of the given trees satisfies the max-heap property

understand.plain: A max-heap requires every parent to be >= its children. We must also check the structural property: a binary heap must be a complete binary tree (all levels fully filled except possibly the last, which is filled from left to right). We need to find the option where both properties hold.

keywords.explain: Every parent node must have a value greater than or equal to both its children.
keywords.example: Parent 10 with children 7 and 3 satisfies max-heap; parent 5 with child 8 does not.

keywords.explain: All levels are completely filled except possibly the last level, which is filled from left to right with no gaps.
keywords.example: A tree with 4 nodes: root has 2 children, left child has 1 left child — this is complete.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='480' height='200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><text x='10' y='20' class='tag ink'>Option A (correct max-heap)</text><line x1='120' y1='50' x2='70' y2='90' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='120' y1='50' x2='170' y2='90' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='90' x2='45' y2='140' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='90' x2='95' y2='140' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='170' y1='90' x2='145' y2='140' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='170' y1='90' x2='195' y2='140' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='120' cy='45' r='22' class='ok'/><text x='120' y='51' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl' style='fill:#fff'>97</text><circle cx='70' cy='90' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='70' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>17</text><circle cx='170' cy='90' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='170' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>46</text><circle cx='45' cy='140' r='18' class='bg'/><text x='45' y='146' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>6</text><circle cx='95' cy='140' r='18' class='bg'/><text x='95' y='146' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>3</text><circle cx='145' cy='140' r='18' class='bg'/><text x='145' y='146' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>12</text><circle cx='195' cy='140' r='18' class='bg'/><text x='195' y='146' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>10</text><text x='240' y='100' class='mut sub'>97>17>6,3 ✓</text><text x='240' y='120' class='mut sub'>97>46>12,10 ✓</text><text x='240' y='140' class='mut sub'>Complete tree ✓</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Tree A: root 97 with left subtree rooted at 17 (children 6, 3) and right subtree rooted at 46 (children 12, 10). All parent-child relationships satisfy max-heap property and the tree is a complete binary tree.

given.aim: Identify which tree satisfies both the max-heap ordering property and the complete binary tree structural property

terms.meaning: The root of all four options is 97, which is correct as the maximum element
terms.example: 97 must be >= all other elements to be the root of a max-heap
terms.connects: Root satisfies max-heap in all options; we must check lower levels

terms.meaning: For every node, its value must be >= its children's values
terms.example: Node 17 with children 6 and 3: 17 >= 6 and 17 >= 3, so it satisfies the property
terms.connects: This must hold for every internal node in the correct option

terms.meaning: Nodes must be placed level by level, left to right, with no gaps
terms.example: If a node has a right child it must also have a left child
terms.connects: Some options may violate structure even if ordering is correct

given.plan: For each option, verify: (1) every parent >= its children (max-heap property), and (2) the tree is a complete binary tree. The one satisfying both is the answer.

steps.title: Check Option A
steps.formula_raw: parent >= left_child AND parent >= right_child for all nodes
steps.apply: Tree A: 97 >= 17 ✓, 97 >= 46 ✓; 17 >= 6 ✓, 17 >= 3 ✓; 46 >= 12 ✓, 46 >= 10 ✓. All 7 nodes placed at levels 1, 2, 3 from left to right — complete binary tree ✓. Both properties satisfied.
steps.note: Option A is a valid max-heap with 7 nodes forming a perfect binary tree.

steps.title: Check Options B, C, D for violations
steps.apply: Option B: node 6 has a right child 7 but no left child is specified before it — violates complete binary tree structure (gap). Option C: 17's left child is 12 > 17's parent relation needs checking; also structural completeness may be violated. Option D: 17 has both left child 5 and right child 12, but 12 appears also in 46's subtree — structural and ordering issues exist. In Option C, 46 has children 6 and 10, and 17 has children 12 and 5 — but 17 < 12 violates max-heap since parent 17 must be >= child 12. Eliminated. Thus only A satisfies both properties.
steps.note: Key elimination: Option B violates completeness; Option C has 17 < 12 (parent < child); Option D has structural inconsistencies.

solution.result: Option A is a valid max-heap — it satisfies both the ordering property (every parent >= children) and the complete binary tree structure.

questions.formula_note: Max-heap validity requires two conditions: (1) heap-order property — every parent >= its children at all levels, and (2) structural property — the tree must be a complete binary tree.

questions.question:
A priority queue is implemented as a Max-Heap. Initially, it has 5 elements. The level-order traversal of the heap is: 10, 8, 5, 3, 2. Two new elements 1 and 7 are inserted into the heap in that order. The level-order traversal of the heap after the insertion of the elements is:

A. 10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 1, 5
B. 10, 8, 7, 2, 3, 1, 5
C. 10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 5, 1
D. 10, 8, 5, 3, 2, 1, 7
questions.to_find: Level-order traversal of the max-heap after inserting elements 1 and 7

understand.plain: We have a max-heap [10, 8, 5, 3, 2] and we insert 1 first, then 7. Each insertion adds the new element at the end of the array (next available position in the complete binary tree) and then sifts it up to restore the max-heap property. We need to find the final level-order (array) representation.

keywords.explain: Add the new element at the last position in the array, then compare it with its parent; if it is greater, swap and continue upward until the heap property is restored.
keywords.example: Insert 9 into [10, 5, 7]: array becomes [10, 5, 7, 9]; 9 > parent 5, swap → [10, 9, 7, 5].

keywords.explain: Since a heap is stored as an array, the level-order traversal is simply the array itself read left to right.
keywords.example: Array [10, 8, 5, 3, 2] corresponds to the heap tree read level by level.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='520' height='260' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe y2='90' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='90' x2='40' y2='140' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='90' x2='100' y2='140' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='190' y1='90' x2='160' y2='140' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='130' cy='40' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='130' y='46' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>10</text><circle cx='70' cy='90' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='70' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>8</text><circle cx='190' cy='90' r='20' class='bg'/><text x='190' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>5</text><circle cx='40' cy='140' r='18' class='bg'/><text x='40' y='146' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>3</text><circle cx='100' cy='140' r='18' class='bg'/><text x='100' y='146' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>2</text><circle cx='160' cy='140' r='18' class='hi'/><text x='160' y='146' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>1</text><text x='260' y='50' class='mut sub'>Insert 1 at pos 6</text><text x='260' y='70' class='mut sub'>1 < parent 5: no swap</text><text x='5' y='185' class='tag ink'>After insert 7: [10,8,5,3,2,1] + 7</text><text x='5' y='205' class='mut sub'>7 at pos 7, parent=5 at pos 3</text><text x='5' y='225' class='mut sub'>7 > 5: swap → [10,8,7,3,2,1,5]</text><text x='5' y='245' class='mut sub'>7's new parent=10: 7 < 10, stop</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Step-by-step heap insertions: first insert 1 (no sift-up needed since 1 < 5), then insert 7 which sifts up past 5 to become the right child of 10, giving final array [10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 1, 5].

given.aim: Determine the level-order traversal of the max-heap after inserting 1 and then 7 into the heap [10, 8, 5, 3, 2]

terms.meaning: A 5-element max-heap where 10 is root, 8 and 5 are level-2 nodes, and 3 and 2 are level-3 nodes
terms.connects: Starting state before the two insertions

terms.meaning: Append 1 at position 6. Its parent is at position 3 which holds value 5. Since 1 < 5, no sift-up needed.
Append 7 at position 7. Its parent is at position 3 which holds value 5. Since 7 > 5, swap. Now 7 is at position 3; its parent is at position 1 which holds 10. Since 7 < 10, stop.
terms.connects: Second insertion results in the final heap [10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 1, 5]

given.plan: Insert 1 at the end of the array and sift up (compare with parent, swap if greater, repeat). Then insert 7 at the end and sift up similarly. Record the final array order.

steps.title: Insert element 1 into the heap
steps.apply: Current heap (1-indexed): [10, 8, 5, 3, 2]. Append 1 at position 6 → [10, 8, 5, 3, 2, 1]. Parent of position 6 is at floor(6/2) = 3, which holds value 5. Since 1 < 5, no swap needed. Heap remains [10, 8, 5, 3, 2, 1].
steps.note: Inserting a small element at the end never requires sifting up.

steps.title: Insert element 7 into the heap
steps.apply: Current heap: [10, 8, 5, 3, 2, 1]. Append 7 at position 7 → [10, 8, 5, 3, 2, 1, 7]. Parent of position 7 is at floor(7/2) = 3, which holds value 5. Since 7 > 5, swap positions 7 and 3 → [10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 1, 5]. Now 7 is at position 3. Parent of position 3 is at floor(3/2) = 1, which holds value 10. Since 7 < 10, stop. Final heap: [10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 1, 5].
steps.note: The level-order traversal of the final heap is 10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 1, 5 — matching option A.

solution.result: The level-order traversal after inserting 1 and 7 is 10, 8, 7, 3, 2, 1, 5 — option A.

questions.formula_note: Heap insertion uses sift-up (bubble-up): append new element at the end, then repeatedly swap with parent while the new element is greater than its parent, until heap property is restored.

questions.question:
Consider a max heap, represented by the array: 40, 30, 20, 10, 15, 16, 17, 8, 4.

Now consider that a value 35 is inserted into this heap. After insertion, the new heap is:

A. 40, 30, 20, 10, 15, 16, 17, 8, 4, 35
B. 40, 35, 20, 10, 30, 16, 17, 8, 4, 15
C. 40, 35, 20, 10, 30, 16, 17, 8, 4, 15 (same)
D. 40, 30, 20, 35, 15, 16, 17, 8, 4, 10
questions.to_find: Array representation of the max-heap after inserting 35 into the given heap

understand.plain: We have a max-heap [40, 30, 20, 10, 15, 16, 17, 8, 4] and we insert 35. The new element is added at the end (position 10) and then sifted up: compare with parent, swap if the new value is larger, continue upward until no more swaps are needed.

keywords.explain: After appending the new element at the end, compare it with its parent. If it is larger, swap them. Repeat with the new parent until the new element is smaller than its parent or it reaches the root.
keywords.example: Insert 35 at position 10, parent at floor(10/2)=5 holds 15. 35>15, swap.

keywords.explain: The parent of node at index i is at index floor(i/2) in a 1-indexed array representation of a binary heap.
keywords.example: Node at index 5 has parent at index 2; node at index 2 has parent at index 1 (root).

class='sub ink'>16</text><circle cx='410' cy='138' r='19' class='bg'/><text x='410' y='144' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>17</text><circle cx='85' cy='188' r='17' class='bg'/><text x='85' y='194' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>8</text><circle cx='135' cy='188' r='17' class='bg'/><text x='135' y='194' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>4</text><text x='5' y='235' class='tag ink'>Insert 35: append at pos 10 (child of pos 5=15)</text><text x='5' y='255' class='mut sub'>35>15: swap → pos5=35,pos10=15 | 35>30: swap → pos2=35,pos5=30 | 35<40: stop</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Max-heap tree before insertion. Node at position 5 holds 15 (left child of 30). Inserting 35: append at position 10, sift up past 15 (swap) then past 30 (swap), stopping below 40. Result: [40, 35, 20, 10, 30, 16, 17, 8, 4, 15].

given.aim: Find the array contents of the max-heap after inserting 35 into [40, 30, 20, 10, 15, 16, 17, 8, 4]

terms.connects: Starting state; 35 will be inserted as the 10th element

terms.example: Position 10's parent is floor(10/2) = 5, which holds value 15

terms.meaning: 35 > 15 at index 5: swap, so 35 moves to index 5. Then 35 > 30 at index 2: swap, so 35 moves to index 2. Then 35 < 40 at index 1: stop.
terms.example: Path: idx10 → idx5 → idx2 (stops before idx1)
terms.connects: Determines the final position of 35 and which elements get displaced

given.plan: Append 35 at position 10. Repeatedly compare with parent and swap if 35 is larger, tracing the sift-up path until 35 is smaller than its parent or becomes the root.

steps.title: Append 35 and check against parent at index 5
steps.apply: Array after append: [40, 30, 20, 10, 15, 16, 17, 8, 4, 35]. Parent of index 10 is index 5 which holds 15. Since 35 > 15, swap indices 5 and 10. Array: [40, 30, 20, 10, 35, 16, 17, 8, 4, 15].
steps.note: 35 has moved to index 5; 15 has moved to index 10.

steps.title: Check 35 (now at index 5) against parent at index 2
steps.apply: Parent of index 5 is index 2 which holds 30. Since 35 > 30, swap indices 2 and 5. Array: [40, 35, 20, 10, 30, 16, 17, 8, 4, 15]. Now 35 is at index 2. Parent of index 2 is index 1 which holds 40. Since 35 < 40, no swap. Sift-up complete.

solution.result: After inserting 35, the max-heap array is [40, 35, 20, 10, 30, 16, 17, 8, 4, 15] — option B.

questions.formula_note: Heap insertion: append at end of array, then sift-up (bubble-up) by repeatedly swapping with parent at floor(i/2) while the inserted value is greater than its parent.
